title: Physician-led trichology: scope, evidence tiers, and ethics
description: Define physician-led clinical trichology versus non-medical hair practice; relate trichology to dermatology and transplantation; apply academy evidence tiers (including regenerative and investigational domains); and uphold consent, scope, and medico-legal boundaries as set out in Volume 1 — Foundations of Clinical Trichology.

evidenceRequirements.description: Pass the Module 1 knowledge check integrating scope, evidence framing, and ethical boundaries (case discussion faculty-moderated where academy workflow requires)

title: Follicle biology, cycling, and scalp environment
description: Explain follicular anatomy, stem-cell niches, hair cycling (including kenogen/exogen), shaft architecture, scalp microenvironment, ethnic variation, and aging-related change as clinical foundations for diagnosis, counselling, and procedural planning per Volume 1.

evidenceRequirements.description: Pass the Module 2 assessment including image-interpretation and case-reasoning items aligned with biology and cycle concepts

title: Molecular and hormonal drivers of hair loss and therapy logic
description: Integrate androgen biology, Wnt and prostaglandin signalling, growth mediators, microinflammation, and sex-specific endocrine contributors into a reasoning framework for AGA, female pattern loss, and adjunctive or regenerative options without overclaiming, per Volume 1.

evidenceRequirements.description: Pass the Module 3 knowledge check on molecular, hormonal, and treatment-sequence logic

title: Structured hair loss consultation and history-taking
description: Conduct a hypothesis-driven consultation: classify complaint type (shedding, density loss, breakage, patchy/scarring); map onset, tempo, and pattern; obtain mandatory female endocrine and reproductive history; identify drug, nutritional, and trigger factors; document psychosocial burden and uncertainty clearly for longitudinal care and governance, per Volume 1.

evidenceRequirements.description: Pass the Module 4 assessment including case-reasoning on history-based differentials, red flags, and documentation (faculty review on free-text items where configured)

title: Clinical examination of hair and scalp
description: Perform a reproducible examination: global pattern recognition, density versus calibre, scalp inflammatory and scarring clues, pull test and shed versus breakage logic, and standardised photography and grading aligned to Volume 2 — Diagnostic Trichology and Pattern Hair Loss Medicine.

evidenceRequirements.description: Pass the Module 5 knowledge check on examination, photography, and red-flag reasoning

title: Trichoscopy: technique, patterns, and biopsy targeting
description: Use dry and wet trichoscopy with comparator logic; recognise pattern hair loss features, shedding and immune-mediated patterns, and scarring clues; report findings clearly; and select informative biopsy margins when histology is indicated, per Volume 2.

evidenceRequirements.description: Pass the Module 6 trichoscopy knowledge check

title: Laboratory investigations and biopsy logic
description: Apply phenotype-led investigation; interpret core laboratories in context; target endocrine workup when clinically indicated; decide when biopsy is required; select site and communicate effectively with pathology; integrate histology with trichoscopy and history, per Volume 2.

evidenceRequirements.description: Pass the Module 7 investigations and biopsy case-reasoning assessment

title: Male androgenetic alopecia: medical ladder and surgical planning
description: Diagnose and stage male AGA; recognise overlap disorders; construct evidence-based medical and adjunctive plans with honest stabilisation versus regrowth framing; assess surgical candidacy, donor risk, and long-term hairline realism, per Volume 2.

evidenceRequirements.description: Pass the Module 8 male AGA knowledge check

title: Female pattern and hormonal hair loss
description: Recognise FPHL phenotypes and overlap with shedding and endocrine disease; investigate proportionately; build reproductive-safe treatment strategies; position adjunctive and surgical options cautiously; and communicate for chronic longitudinal care, per Volume 2.

evidenceRequirements.description: Pass the Module 9 female pattern hair loss knowledge check

title: Telogen effluvium and diffuse shedding disorders
description: Differentiate acute and chronic telogen effluvium from anagen shedding and patterned loss; map triggers and timelines; integrate trichoscopy and proportionate investigation; counsel natural history and referral boundaries, per Volume 3 — Diffuse Shedding, Cicatricial Alopecia, and Scalp Disease.

evidenceRequirements.description: Pass the Module 10 knowledge check on shedding biology, overlap disorders, and investigation discipline

title: Alopecia areata and immune-mediated hair loss
description: Recognise AA phenotypes including diffuse and ophiasis patterns; use trichoscopy supportively; differentiate tinea, traction, trichotillomania, and cicatricial mimics; outline evidence-aware management framing without overclaiming emerging therapies, per Volume 3.

evidenceRequirements.description: Pass the Module 11 alopecia areata knowledge check

title: Hair shaft disorders and breakage syndromes
description: Separate shedding from shaft breakage; address weathering, traction, cosmetic injury, and trichotillomania with appropriate referral; avoid mis-investigation when follicles are structurally intact, per Volume 3.

evidenceRequirements.description: Pass the Module 12 shaft and breakage knowledge check

title: Principles of cicatricial alopecia
description: Classify scarring alopecia patterns; recognise clinical and trichoscopic urgency; apply biopsy site philosophy and pathology communication; prioritise disease control before restorative discussion, per Volume 3.

evidenceRequirements.description: Pass the Module 13 cicatricial principles knowledge check

title: Lymphocytic cicatricial alopecias
description: Differentiate LPP, FFA, DLE, and CCCA phenotypes; use trichoscopy and biopsy appropriately; co-manage with dermatology; discuss traction and cultural context without blame; align therapy framing to specialist pathways, per Volume 3.

evidenceRequirements.description: Pass the Module 14 lymphocytic cicatricial knowledge check

title: Neutrophilic and mixed cicatricial alopecias
description: Recognise folliculitis decalvans and dissecting cellulitis; interpret tufting and destructive folliculitis as urgency signals; outline longitudinal multidisciplinary principles without substituting local specialist protocols, per Volume 3.

evidenceRequirements.description: Pass the Module 15 neutrophilic cicatricial knowledge check

title: Inflammatory and infectious scalp disorders
description: Manage seborrhoeic dermatitis and scalp psoriasis in hair consultations; evaluate contact and irritant disease; suspect and treat tinea capitis with scarring awareness; integrate scalp surface control with primary alopecia diagnosis, per Volume 3.

evidenceRequirements.description: Pass the Module 16 inflammatory and infectious scalp knowledge check
